findings.evidence: Line 54 applies `IsProjectiveLimit.unique (P := fun I => μ.map I.restrict)`, whose Mathlib signature at `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Constructions/Projective.lean:151` requires only `[∀ i, IsFiniteMeasure (P i)]`; this comes from `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`, not from `ν`.
findings.fix: Drop `[IsFiniteMeasure ν]` from `measure_eq_of_prefixProj_map_eq` and the derived setwise theorem, and adjust the docstrings from "Two finite measures" accordingly.
findings.issue: `[IsFiniteMeasure ν]` is stronger than needed for `measure_eq_of_prefixProj_map_eq`; once `ν` has the same finite marginals as finite `μ`, projective-limit uniqueness already forces equality.

summary: The prefix uniqueness API is at the intended TauCeti wrapper level, but it carries an unnecessary finiteness assumption on the second measure.
